plan_metadata.name: Feature Prompt Use Run From Package

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and apps" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" selection list.
Click the "Tab" option in the "Select an option" dropdown within the Teams Capability popup in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dropdown at the top of the VS Code window to choose the project root directory.
@code In the file content: \"/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/infra/azure.bicep\", remove  line 32-35. then save the file.
Dismiss the "Notifications" pop-up in Visual Studio Code by pressing the Esc key.
Click the "OUTPUT" tab in the terminal panel at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code interface to switch the view from "TERMINAL" to "OUTPUT".
Click the "Tasks" tab in the Output panel at the bottom right of the Visual Studio Code Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.

Type the letter 'm' into the "Filter" input field within the OUTPUT panel dropdown menu in Visual Studio Code, with the context menu currently displaying log categories such as "Tasks" and "Extension Host".

Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" option in the OUTPUT panel filter dropdown within Visual Studio Code. The red crosshair highlights this menu selection, specifying the output source for viewing logs or messages related to the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Click the search field labeled "Find" in the OUTPUT panel at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code window to activate text search within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit output.
Type text: 'run-from-package' into the "Filter" input box located above the OUTPUT panel in Visual Studio Code within the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT workspace.
@assertion  There is  a link \"https://aka.ms/teamsfx-config-run-from-package\"  in output panel.
